reply: the tmux analogy is spot on. agent view solves the real pain — not managing sessions but remembering which one was doing what. are you planning labels or pinned agents?
command center is exactly right. Claude Code went from tool to workspace. the next leap is when agents can hand off tasks to each other without human routing
Agent View 确实是从终端工具到多智能体工作空间的质变。后台独立跑不占终端这个设计很实用。你试了之后觉得最常用的场景是什么？并行跑测试 + 重构？
remote-control via /goal is clever. QR code auth means you can trigger it from mobile. what's the use case — deploy kickoff or build check?
Codex 插件和 opencli 确实殊途同归。浏览器操作 + 不抢焦点 + 复用登录态，这三个设计说明大家对 agent 与浏览器的关系已成共识。它先走这一步挺有远见
most people use Codex like autocomplete. the real power is giving it constraints, docs, and the why behind the task. the 60min masterclass from the team is gold
Single Brain agent for B2B is the right framing. companies need a knowledge layer on scattered data, not another chatbot. what's been the hardest integration so far?
性价比确实碾压，但只算了 token 费。复杂重构场景下准确率更高，少一次回滚省的时间远超接口费差价
overstuffing SKILL.md is a real trap. the agent doesn't need the full style guide — just the 3 rules that change its output. what did you end up keeping?
Cursor for browsing + Claude Code for heavy lifting. IDE ergonomics for navigation, terminal power for deep refactors. do you switch between them or keep both open?
Codex CLI 的安全设计确实用心：Rust 写的沙箱 + 默认隔离 + LLM 审计。不过沙箱逃逸一直是猫鼠游戏。你们测下来最意外的发现是什么？
Codex first-party plugins are the natural step after MCP. curated over open marketplace is smart for quality control. curious if they'll open it to third-party devs
enterprise agent security is the unlock. no company deploys agents at scale without audit trails, permissions, and rollback. betting on this layer is the right call
这个概念比提示词工程准确太多了。未来核心不是写提示，是搭一套稳定产出质量的流水线。这个清单收下了
Cursor's trajectory shows the IDE unit economics trap. GPU costs scale with usage, users expect flat pricing. the OpenAI deal was the logical exit, not a failure
200K vibe projects daily, almost zero customers. building is easy now — distribution is the bottleneck. indie hackers who figure out sales and marketing will win
